stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 25 factory workers. The sample mean is 93.9 sec with a sample standard deviation of 35.3 sec. However, 2 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 23 observations have a mean of 91.0 sec and standard deviation of 26.1 sec. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 93.9, 95% CI = [79.3, 108.5]. Without outliers: mean = 91.0, 95% CI = [80.1, 101.9]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [79.3, 108.5]. Point estimate: 92.5 sec.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [79.3, 108.5].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
